title: Neocheiropteris palmatopedata
taxonomy: Neocheiropteris palmatopedata belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It is classified under the division Pteridophyta, which includes ferns. Further classification places it under the order Polypodiales, with its family Polypodiaceae being a part of this order. The genus Neocheiropteris is a group of ferns that are native to tropical Asia. Within the genus Neocheiropteris, Neocheiropteris palmatopedata is one of the species.
uses: Neocheiropteris palmatopedata has been found to have various medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. intro: Neocheiropteris palmatopedata, also known as Chinese maidenhair fern, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Neocheiropteris palmatopedata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Neocheiropteris palmatopedata include tea, capsules, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory tract infections and other health conditions.
